children.heading: Authority of Secretary
children.label: a Authority of Secretary
children.text: The Secretary may enter into contracts, financial assistance agreements, and such other agreements, and promulgate such regulations, as are necessary to carry out this subchapter.

children.heading: Transfer of projects
children.label: b Transfer of projects
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter authorizes the transfer of pre-existing facilities or pre-existing components of any water system from Federal to private ownership or from private to Federal ownership.

children.heading: Federal reclamation law
children.label: c Federal reclamation law
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter supersedes or amends any Federal law associated with a project, or portion of a project, constructed under Federal reclamation law.

children.heading: Interagency coordination
children.label: d Interagency coordination
children.text: The Secretary shall coordinate the Program carried out under this subchapter with existing Federal and State rural water and wastewater programs to facilitate the most efficient and effective solution to meeting the water needs of the non-Federal project sponsors.

children.heading: Multiple Indian tribes
children.label: e Multiple Indian tribes
children.text: In any case in which a contract is entered into with, or a grant is made, to an organization to perform services benefitting more than 1 Indian tribe under this subchapter, the approval of each such Indian tribe shall be a prerequisite to entering into the contract or making the grant.1So in original. The comma probably should follow “to”.1

children.heading: Ownership of facilities
children.label: f Ownership of facilities
children.text: Title to any facility planned, designed, and recommended for construction under this subchapter shall be held by the non-Federal project entity.

children.heading: Expedited procedures
children.label: g Expedited procedures
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a community to be served by a proposed rural water supply project has urgent and compelling water needs, the Secretary shall, to the maximum extent practicable, expedite appraisal investigations and reports conducted under and feasibility studies and reports conducted under .section 2404 of this titlesection 2405 of this title

children.heading: Effect on State water law
children.label: h Effect on State water law

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter preempts or affects State water law or an interstate compact governing water.

children.heading: Compliance required
children.label: 2 Compliance required
children.text: The Secretary shall comply with State water laws in carrying out this subchapter.

children.heading: No additional requirements
children.label: i No additional requirements
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter requires a feasibility study for, or imposes any other additional requirements with respect to, rural water supply projects or programs that are authorized before .December 22, 2006
